{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shirageyama Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, funcho.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg| </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Shirageyama Kofun}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E|region:JP-26|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers

The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11580285]] Wikidata name: Shirageyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492944|N|135.100806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580285]] Wikidata name: Shirageyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492944|N|135.100806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shirageyama Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, funcho.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg| </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580285]] Wikidata name: Shirageyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492944|N|135.100806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shirageyama Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, funcho.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg| </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580285]] Wikidata name: Shirageyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492944|N|135.100806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shirageyama Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, funcho.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg| </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]

}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580285]] Wikidata name: Shirageyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492944|N|135.100806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580285]] Wikidata name: Shirageyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492944|N|135.100806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shirageyama Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, funcho.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg| </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]

}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirageyama Kofun]][[en:Shirageyama Kofun]][[ja:白米山古墳]][[zh:白米山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580285]] Wikidata name: Shirageyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492944|N|135.100806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for [[:en:Japan National Route 176|Japan National Route 176]] further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shirageyama Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, funcho.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg| </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]

name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|WD=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for {{ill|Japan National Route 176|en|Japan National Route 176|WD=Q11421416|lt=Japan National Route 176}} further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers

The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers

The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|WD=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for {{ill|Japan National Route 176|en|Japan National Route 176|WD=Q11421416|lt=Japan National Route 176}} further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for {{ill|Japan National Route 176|en|Japan National Route 176|WD=Q11421416|lt=Japan National Route 176|1=Japan National Route 176}} further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shirageyama Kofun | native_name =白米山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shirageyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shirageyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492944|N|135.100806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|WD=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto|1=Yosano, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|34.40|N|135|06|2.9|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Shirageyama Kofun'''|白米山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Ushirono neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|WD=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto|1=Yosano, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus which was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203476 |title=白米山古墳 |trans-title= Shirageyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shirageyama Kofun is located on a small hill with a relative elevation of about 20 meters from the plain, overlooking the middle reaches of the Noda River, which flows into Miyazu Bay, (where [[:en:Ama-no-Hashidate|Ama-no-Hashidate]] is located) at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. The [[Ebisuyama Kofun]] and [[Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)|Tsukuriyama Kofun]] are located about 1.5 kilometers to the north. The Shirageyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus was known since the early twentieth century and was surveyed several times. In 1987, it was designated as a Kyoto Prefectural Historic Site. Due to planned construction work on a bypass for {{ill|Japan National Route 176|en|Japan National Route 176|WD=Q11421416|lt=Japan National Route 176|1=Japan National Route 176}} further surveys for the purposes of conserving the site were conducted from 1996 to 1998. The tumulus has an overall length of 90 meters and is orientated to the north. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' (some of which remains in situ), but trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been found. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber; however, there are also two earthen graves, and one wooden coffin direct burial in the center of the posterior circular portion of the mound. Details of these tombs are uncertain, as a complete [[archaeological excavation]] has not taken place. The tumulus is estimated to date from the middle of the early Kofun period, or early 4th century, based on the earthenware found, which makes it one of the oldest on the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} coast.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 90 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 32 meters wide x 5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 2-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> Shirageyama Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, funcho.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg| Shirageyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg| </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
